runtime_authority: connected backend version and advertised effective capabilities
implementation_authority: versioned code, generated contracts, conformance tests, and tagged release evidence

documents.title: Choose how you want to use Mobazha
documents.summary: Start as a buyer, seller, Node operator, developer, or project evaluator without learning the repository layout first.

knowledge_authority.label: Choose how you want to use Mobazha

documents.outcome: Reach the shortest trustworthy path for the job you want to complete.

documents.title: Choose hosted or self-hosted Mobazha
documents.summary: Choose the operating model from control, responsibility, integrations, and service needs—not from a false free-versus-paid shortcut.

evidence.label: Mobazha public product and release boundaries

documents.title: Use Mobazha documentation as an Agent
documents.summary: Resolve authority, applicability, and user consent before turning documentation into an action.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

knowledge_authority.label: Use Mobazha documentation as an Agent

documents.title: Start and operate a Mobazha store
documents.summary: Move from store setup to listings, payment readiness, fulfillment, and order recovery while keeping operator responsibilities explicit.

knowledge_authority.label: Start and operate a Mobazha store

documents.outcome: Prepare a store that a buyer can understand, pay, and receive from through one tested journey.

documents.title: Prepare a store for its first order
documents.summary: Configure store identity, policies, fulfillment, payments, and a complete test journey before accepting buyer funds.

knowledge_authority.label: Prepare a store for its first order

documents.title: Create and validate a listing
documents.summary: Publish an accurate item with explicit options, price, availability, fulfillment, and buyer-visible terms.

evidence.label: Unified listing editor and Node listing API

documents.title: Configure shipping and delivery
documents.summary: Define where an item can be delivered, how the buyer is charged, and what evidence the seller records after fulfillment.

evidence.label: Unified shipping profiles and Node listing contracts

documents.title: Prepare payment methods for selling
documents.summary: Advertise only payment methods that are allowed, configured, healthy, and operationally monitored by the store backend.

evidence.label: Node payment capabilities and Unified payment administration

documents.title: Operate incoming orders
documents.summary: Reconcile payment, confirm the obligation, record fulfillment evidence, and use only state-valid cancellation, refund, dispute, and completion actions.

evidence.label: Unified order administration and Node order state contracts

documents.title: Buy from an independent Mobazha store
documents.summary: Follow checkout, payment, order tracking, cancellation, refund, and dispute paths against the store that owns the order.

knowledge_authority.label: Buy from an independent Mobazha store

documents.outcome: See who operates the store, what you will pay, and how to recover the order before you commit funds.

documents.title: Complete a buyer checkout
documents.summary: Review one store, one quote, and one payment instruction set before creating or funding an order.

evidence.label: Unified checkout routes and Node order contracts

documents.title: Use Guest Checkout safely
documents.summary: Create an anonymous buyer order while preserving the store routing context and a recoverable tracking link.

evidence.label: Unified Guest Checkout implementation and Node public guest APIs

documents.title: Track payment and order status
documents.summary: Reconcile wallet evidence with the backend-owned order state before deciding that an order is funded, shipped, refundable, or complete.

evidence.label: Unified order milestones and Node order API

documents.title: Cancel, request a refund, or open a dispute
documents.summary: Choose the transition allowed by the current order state and preserve the evidence needed to explain the requested remedy.

knowledge_authority.label: Cancel, request a refund, or open a dispute

documents.title: Run a Mobazha Node under your control
documents.summary: Evaluate requirements, install on testnet, define the network boundary, monitor health, and prepare recovery before production use.

knowledge_authority.label: Run a Mobazha Node under your control

documents.outcome: Decide whether self-hosting fits, then reach a healthy and recoverable evaluation Node.

documents.title: Self-hosting requirements and readiness
documents.summary: Confirm platform, ownership, recovery, network, and operational capacity before installing a Mobazha Node.

evidence.label: Node build and standalone deployment sources

documents.title: Install a Mobazha Node
documents.summary: Build the release candidate from public source, start it locally, and verify the UI and health boundary before exposing it.

documents.title: Configure a self-hosted node
documents.summary: Make deployment mode, data location, network exposure, payments, and external dependencies explicit and recoverable.

evidence.label: Mobazha Node command and deployment sources

documents.title: Design the network and TLS boundary
documents.summary: Keep Node administration private by default and expose only intentionally authenticated, encrypted, and monitored routes.

knowledge_authority.label: Design the network and TLS boundary

documents.title: Monitor a self-hosted Node
documents.summary: Detect process, storage, capability, payment, webhook, and recovery degradation before it becomes an order incident.

documents.title: Back up and upgrade safely
documents.summary: Treat upgrade readiness as a recoverability exercise, not only a package update.

documents.title: Secure a self-hosted node
documents.summary: Protect the host, administrative boundary, signing material, payment integrations, backups, and recovery path as one system.

evidence.label: Mobazha security policy and extension security model

documents.title: Troubleshoot a Mobazha Node
documents.summary: Diagnose version, process, health, capability, configuration, and dependency failures without exposing sensitive data.

documents.title: Connect optional hosted capabilities
documents.summary: Keep local node ownership separate from any optional account or hosted service connection.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

documents.title: Build on advertised capabilities
documents.summary: Integrations should discover what a backend supports instead of assuming every Mobazha deployment exposes the same surface.

documents.title: Call your local Mobazha Node API in five minutes
documents.summary: Discover runtime capabilities, use the declared authentication boundary, and make one read-only request against a local evaluation Node.

knowledge_authority.label: Call your local Mobazha Node API in five minutes

evidence.label: Generated Node OpenAPI and runtime contract

documents.outcome: Confirm the Node you reached and complete one scoped, read-only API request.

documents.title: Authentication, identities, and scopes
documents.summary: Select the identity and credential model declared by the connected deployment and grant only the scopes required for one integration.

evidence.label: OpenAPI security schemes and Node auth token API

documents.title: Errors, retries, and idempotency
documents.summary: Preserve business correctness across transport failures, duplicate delivery, conflicts, timeouts, and unknown outcomes.

evidence.label: Node API error contracts and state-machine implementation

documents.title: Runtime capabilities and product composition
documents.summary: Discover effective behavior from the connected backend and keep deployment, experience, capabilities, permissions, and experiments separate.

documents.title: Token and on-chain asset identifiers
documents.summary: Use a versioned, parseable identifier across clients while treating exact parsing behavior and supported standards as release-scoped contracts.

evidence.label: Unified token identifier implementation and tests

documents.title: HTTP API and OpenAPI
documents.summary: Use the generated Mobazha Node OpenAPI contract as the operation and schema reference, then verify runtime capabilities before calling optional features.

documents.title: WebSocket events
documents.summary: Use authenticated WebSocket events for timely UI updates, then reconcile important state through the authoritative HTTP API.

evidence.label: Mobazha WebSocket gateway and event registry

documents.title: MCP and agent integrations
documents.summary: Agents can discover and invoke permitted commerce capabilities, but cannot replace user consent, policy, or backend authorization.

documents.title: Webhook integration contract
documents.summary: Design consumers for authentication, retries, duplication, reordering, and version change before depending on event delivery.

evidence.label: Mobazha webhook engine and OpenAPI contract

documents.title: Extend Mobazha through public contracts
documents.summary: Choose the narrowest typed extension mechanism and preserve Core authority, capability gates, isolation, recovery, and audit.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

documents.title: Reference and source map
documents.summary: Find the public knowledge authority, runtime authority, contract, and implementation evidence before proposing or automating a change.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Understand the Mobazha project
documents.summary: Separate what ships now from project principles, reviewed policy, historical proposals, and long-term direction.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and project records

documents.title: Architecture and trust boundaries
documents.summary: Mobazha separates user clients, independently operated backends, optional hosted services, and external payment or delivery systems.

documents.title: Founding whitepaper
documents.summary: A public-review account of Mobazha's problem framing, trust model, human-and-Agent interfaces, and approach to sustainable open commerce.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

evidence.label: Public-review edition prepared for the Mobazha project

documents.title: Fees and sustainable economics
documents.summary: Mobazha does not define one unavoidable commission for every transaction. Each actual charge must identify its owner, purpose, basis, and point of consent.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

evidence.label: Mobazha public implementation and release evidence

documents.title: Compatibility policy
documents.summary: Compatibility covers wire contracts, state semantics, capabilities, versioning, extensions, migration, and conformance—not matching names alone.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

evidence.label: Node public contracts and conformance sources

documents.title: OEM, appliance, and VPS distribution policy
documents.summary: Distributors may package Mobazha with services while preserving source obligations, capability boundaries, secure first run, user control, and honest branding.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

knowledge_authority.label: OEM, appliance, and VPS distribution policy

documents.title: Public product roadmap
documents.summary: Track product outcomes and maturity without converting internal estimates, implementation checklists, or experiments into delivery promises.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

evidence.label: Public repository milestones and implementation roadmaps

documents.title: Project security model
documents.summary: Security depends on explicit authority, fail-closed capabilities, protected signing material, hostile-input assumptions, and private disclosure.

documents.title: Legal, privacy, license, and trademark boundaries
documents.summary: Separate software licenses, hosted-service terms, privacy commitments, third-party obligations, and trademark rights.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

knowledge_authority.label: Legal, privacy, license, and trademark boundaries

evidence.label: Mobazha public policies and repository notices

documents.title: Release scope and maturity
documents.summary: The documentation distinguishes current release-candidate behavior from previews and future design.

documents.title: Documentation and policy governance
documents.summary: Important project claims need an owner, source, review date, status, and visible change path.
documents.applies_to: Design direction; not a shipped guarantee

evidence.label: Mobazha public governance and documentation policy

documents.title: Public decisions and proposals
documents.summary: Use RFCs for changes still being evaluated, ADRs for decisions already made, and history records for superseded public material.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Requests for Comment
documents.summary: Review substantial protocol, policy, economic, security, governance, and cross-repository proposals before treating them as commitments.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Architecture Decision Records
documents.summary: Preserve why durable technical and product decisions were made, what alternatives were rejected, and what would supersede them.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: History and supersession
documents.summary: Preserve replaced public proposals and guidance without allowing historical text to masquerade as current behavior or policy.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Releases
documents.summary: Use repository releases for exact versions, migration notes, checksums, and known issues.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Contribute to Mobazha
documents.summary: Choose the owning repository, agree on large changes early, preserve public contracts, add tests, update documentation, and sign off commits.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: Get help and report problems
documents.summary: Use public support for reproducible product questions, repository issues for defects, and private reporting for security problems.
documents.applies_to: Current public project policy or service surface

documents.title: 从你要完成的任务开始
documents.summary: Mobazha 是一套开放商业技术栈，可用于自行托管商店、托管服务、市场、结账和 Agent 自动化。

knowledge_authority.label: 从你要完成的任务开始

documents.title: 选择托管服务或自行托管
documents.summary: 根据控制权、运维责任、集成需求和服务价值选择，而不是把它简化成“免费或收费”。

knowledge_authority.label: 选择托管服务或自行托管

documents.title: Agent 如何使用 Mobazha 文档

knowledge_authority.label: Agent 如何使用 Mobazha 文档

documents.title: 通过 Mobazha 开店与销售
documents.summary: 从店铺准备、商品发布、报价与付款，到订单履约和售后，保持责任与成本透明。

documents.title: 在条款与成本透明的前提下购买

documents.title: 运行自己的 Mobazha Node
documents.summary: 自行托管让店铺数据与运营处于你的控制之下，同时把安全、备份、升级和可用性责任交给你。

documents.title: 安装 Mobazha Node
documents.summary: 从公开源码构建候选版本，在本地和测试网启动，并在对外暴露前验证 UI 与运行边界。

documents.title: 基于后端实际公开的能力进行开发

documents.title: 运行时能力与产品组合

documents.title: Token 与链上资产标识
documents.summary: 在客户端之间使用带版本、可解析的标识，同时让精确解析行为和支持标准继续受发布版本契约约束。

documents.title: 通过公共契约扩展 Mobazha
documents.summary: 选择最窄的类型化扩展机制，并保留 Core 权威、能力门槛、运行隔离、恢复和审计边界。
